_comment: The Review-PRs prompt model: depth levels + the scope/action text blocks that the wizard assembles into the agent prompt. Verbatim from ReviewWizard.swift. The assembly order/conditions live as a tiny glue layer in each front-end.

depths.title: Quick static pass
depths.blurb: Read each diff, flag obvious issues. Nothing run.
depths.fragment: Do a QUICK STATIC review of each PR: read the full diff and the surrounding code it touches, and flag obvious bugs, regressions, scope creep and bad practices. You don't need to run or reproduce anything — this is a fast read-through pass.

depths.title: Standard swarm
depths.blurb: Swarm per PR across the review lenses; verify findings.
depths.fragment: For each PR, dispatch a swarm of review agents (3–8) covering the standard lenses — correctness, scope/simplification, edge cases (nulls, boundaries, errors, concurrency) and ripple effects on callers/docs/tests. Treat every finding as a lead, not a verdict: verify it against the actual code before acting, then fix the real ones directly on the PR's branch.

depths.title: Deep · hard repro
depths.blurb: Swarm + concrete repro before & after every on-branch fix.
depths.fragment: For each PR, dispatch swarms of agents across the review lenses. For EVERY suspected issue, build a concrete, hard reproduction that proves it real BEFORE touching anything; then fix it directly on the PR's branch and re-run the same reproduction to confirm the fix lands. Keep dispatching fresh swarms at each PR until a full pass turns up nothing left to fix.

depths.title: Full E2E ×2
depths.blurb: Run each PR E2E; second independent pass; repro-verified.
depths.fragment: Maximum rigor. For each PR: run it END-TO-END through its real entry point and confirm observable behaviour — not just that it compiles. Dispatch swarms across every review lens; every finding gets a concrete hard reproduction before the fix and the SAME repro re-run after to prove the fix lands. Then do a SECOND, independent verification pass over the PR to catch anything the first missed. Fix directly on the branches, and keep dispatching swarms until TWO consecutive passes come back completely clean.

scope.single: Review PR #{pr} in {owner}/{repo}. Use the `gh` CLI to fetch it.
scope.multi: Go through {scope} in {owner}/{repo}. Use the `gh` CLI to enumerate them.

blocks.bar: Hold to the bar in my CLAUDE.md throughout: prove every issue beyond reasonable doubt with a concrete reproduction before you act on it, scale the swarm to the work, and never report something fixed without re-running the repro to confirm it landed.
blocks.markReady: If a PR turns out perfectly clean — no issues, regressions or bad practices left — mark it ready for review and report its number to me. List every PR you cleared at the end.
blocks.leaveReviews: Leave a formal GitHub review on each PR (POST a pull-request review, not a top-level comment), following the review-comment rules in my CLAUDE.md: one inline per-line comment per finding anchored to the exact line(s), describe the problem and its concrete impact only (never propose the fix), strip every internal severity/category marking from the text, and never leave an LGTM / "no issues" comment.
blocks.reply: For review threads that OTHERS have left on these PRs: address each one, and never mark a thread resolved without first replying "Fixed in <commit_hash>" with the real commit hash.
blocks.trailer: Keep dispatching swarms until every PR you go through comes back clean. No AI attribution anywhere in git/GitHub — commits authored as me, no Co-Authored-By, no "Generated with" taglines.
